claims.claim: In the injected-failure scenario, the AST runner passed 5 of 5 governance checks (dependency blocking, budget enforcement, rollback execution, typed evidence emission, and drift prevention), while a naive linear baseline passed only 1 of 3 checks — detecting the failure but proceeding to execute a dependent step and leaving a stale artifact.

evidence_refs.quote: Long-running autonomous agents risk drift when execution proceeds linearly past failed quality gates, accumulating stale partial artifacts and violating budget constraints. We investigate whether representing agent plans as executable abstract syntax trees (ASTs) — with explicit dependency edges, conditional guards, loop expansion, budget enforcement, and rollback stacks — can mitigate this drift relative to naive linear command lists.
